bids.reasoning: Since this is the opening offer at the very beginning of the negotiation, I will propose a bid that maximizes my utility to establish a strong anchor. I am targeting my most preferred values across all issues, particularly on my highest-weighted issues F, E, and B, while I leave room for future concessions on my lower-weighted issues.

The negotiation has just begun, but I can already see that we stand in full agreement on issueD (valueB) and issueE (valueA). I will lock in these mutually beneficial values. To show early willingness to compromise, I will also concede on issueA, matching the opponent's request (valueA), since doing so causes only a minor drop in my utility while maintaining my preferred values for the higher-weighted issues.

The negotiation is still very early (t=0.04), so I am comfortably holding my most important issues (issueF, issueE, and issueB) at my highest utility values while exploring concessions on lower-weight issues. The opponent is holding firm on their initial offer, which already grants my highest utility for issueD and issueE. I will repeat their preferred value for issueA and try a small concession on issueC to break the deadlock and encourage them to move on issueB or issueF.

The opponent has repeated the same offer three times. Since time is still very early (t=0.06), I will avoid steep concessions on my highest-priority issues (issueF and issueB). However, to show flexibility and break the deadlock, I will fully concede on issueC and issueA to match their exact requested values, while keeping my most critical issues optimized for myself. This offer aligns our positions on issueA, issueC, issueD, and issueE.

The current time fraction is very low (t=0.08). The opponent has repeatedly proposed the same offer without conceding anything. To encourage movement from the opponent while protecting my most important issues (F, E, and B), I will propose an offer that retains my highest utility values for these key issues, but offers a partial concession on issue C to show flexibility.

The negotiation is still in the early stages (t=0.1). The opponent has consistently shown interest in issueE: valueA, which aligns with my own highest utility for that issue, and recently shifted to issueA: valueB and issueD: valueA. I will concede completely on issues A and D to match their latest move, while keeping my most heavily weighted issues (B, C, and F) at my maximum utility values to ensure a total utility over 0.8.

The negotiation is still very early (t=0.12), so I will hold firm on my most important issues, primarily issue F, issue E, and issue B. The opponent has consistently requested E: valueA, which aligns with my top preference, so we share that value. I am willing to offer valueA for issueA and valueA for issueD to show some flexibility, while maintaining my highly valued choices for the other issues to preserve overall utility.

The negotiation is in the early stages (t=0.14). The opponent clearly desires `issueD: valueA` and `issueC: valueA`. Since `issueD` carries no weight for me, and `issueC` carries a low weight, I will happily accommodate these values to demonstrate willingness to cooperate. In exchange, I am holding firm on my most important issues (`issueB`, `issueE`, `issueF`) and returning `issueA` to `valueC` to maintain my utility above 0.8.

The current time fraction is 0.16, meaning it is still early in the negotiation process, allowing me to maintain a high-utility target. The opponent continues to offer `valueA` for `issueE` (which maximizes my utility on that issue) and just reverted to my most preferred `valueB` for `issueD`. I will propose a bid that embraces these shared values while holding firm on my high-priority issues (`issueF` and `issueB`), yielding a strong utility of approximately 0.86.

The negotiation is still in the early stages (t=0.18). The opponent has made a small concession by shifting issueF to valueB, but issueF is my most important issue so I will firmly hold onto valueC. I will counter with an offer that accommodates their preferences for issueA (valueA), issueD (valueB), and issueE (valueA), but preserves my highest utility values on my most critical issues (issueB, issueE, and issueF).

The opponent significantly lowered my utility in their last offer by shifting issueE to valueB, though they previously accepted valueA for it. Since it is still early in the negotiation (t=0.2), I will maintain a high utility target (>0.80) by standing firm on my most heavily weighted issues (issueF, issueE, and issueB). However, I will accommodate their clear preference for issueC=valueA and issueD=valueB to make the proposal more appealing to them.

The opponent's most recent offer yields a utility of 0.786 for me, giving me my top choices on my most important issues (issueB, issueE, and issueF). Given this extremely favorable proposal at an early stage of the negotiation (t=0.22), it is advantageous to accept it immediately to avoid any risk of the opponent retreating from these significant concessions. I am submitting the exact same offer to close the deal.
